tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Generosity Rewarded: Vietnamese Café Owner Receives Home After a Lifetime of Giving
stories.summary: Lieng Le, an 86-year-old Vietnamese café owner who was sleeping in a foldout chair at her restaurant, received a home after a viral video sparked a $200,000 fundraiser.

stories.headline: Stephen Curry Lights Up a Young Fan's Day With a Personal Game Invite
stories.summary: Twelve-year-old Wyatt, who has Down syndrome, banked a shot from Curry range in his Curry jersey, prompting Stephen Curry to personally invite him to a Warriors game.

stories.headline: From Doubt to History: Polish Underdog Reaches Roland Garros Final
stories.summary: Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska, ranked 114th, became the first female qualifier ever to reach a Roland Garros final after winning just two career tour-level clay victories.

stories.headline: Forty Strangers Transform a Mother's Home in 48 Hours
stories.summary: More than 40 volunteers from Project 33 transformed Sarah Walton's home in 48 hours to meet the needs of her three sons, aged 8, 9, and 12.

stories.headline: Theater's Heart: 85-Year-Old Worker Receives $146K in Donations for 45 Years of Dedication
stories.summary: Mary Ellen Eron, an 85-year-old AMC theater worker in Tennessee with 45 years of service, received $146,317 from over 7,500 donors after a viral video of her dedication.

stories.headline: Hope Takes Flight: Four Rare Guam Kingfisher Chicks Hatch at Virginia Sanctuary
stories.summary: First-time parents Poki and Antonio welcomed four Guam kingfisher chicks at Virginia's Smithsonian sanctuary, boosting a species with only 125 individuals left in the world.

stories.headline: Young Friends Create Kindness Kits to Brighten Hospital Days
stories.summary: Two boys organized their school's Kindness Club to create and donate care packages for hospitalized children battling cancer.

stories.headline: A Champion's Comfort: Marquinhos Lifts Up Gabriel After Penalty Miss
stories.summary: After Gabriel missed the deciding penalty in the Champions League final, compatriot Marquinhos comforted him, drawing on his own heartbreak.

stories.headline: Community Builds Wooden Waterwheel as Veterans' Tribute
stories.summary: Neighbors donated 1,300 hours of labor to craft a beautiful oak waterwheel, creating a lasting tribute to veterans in Marshfield.

stories.headline: Divers Unite to Save Coral Reefs, Removing 1,300 Destructive Starfish
stories.summary: Community divers from four Malaysian islands removed 1,318 crown-of-thorns starfish in two days to protect threatened coral reefs.

stories.headline: Scientists Discover Antibiotic That Conquers Drug-Resistant Bacteria
stories.summary: McMaster Professor Gerry Wright's team discovered manikomycin, the fourth new antibiotic candidate from his lab in just over a year, targeting drug-resistant bacteria like E. coli and Salmonella.

stories.headline: New Treatment Cuts Relapse Risk by Half in Rare Immune Disorder
stories.summary: Stanford Medicine researchers found obexelimab cuts relapse risk in half for patients with IgG4-related disease, a rare chronic immune condition often misdiagnosed as cancer.

stories.headline: Lab-Grown Heart Patches Show Promise for Heart Failure Patients
stories.summary: Bioengineered heart muscle patches from stem cells demonstrate encouraging early signs of healing and improving cardiac function in advanced heart failure patients.
